title: centaurea diomedea
intro: Centaurea diomedea, also known as Centaury, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall, upright stems and clusters of small, yellow flowers. Traditionally, Centaury has been used in herbal medicine for its diuretic and expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is believed to possess various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Centaury is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
